research_sources.grant_use: Master index for work packages, evidence categories, and sensitive-finance exclusion policy.
Existing IFS language controls and claim-class definitions.
Local source hierarchy for theory, evidence analytics, hardware, and validation material.
Validation, falsification, replication and measurement materials for careful feasibility framing.
Benchmark, claims-and-evidence, analytics and public-metrics material for narrative support after claim review.
Architecture, hardware sketches, signal-processing and quantum-adjacent research material; not proof of an external hardware lead.
Research whitepapers and theoretical framing; must be expressed as hypothesis or early-stage feasibility unless externally validated.
Handwritten internal operator architecture notes page 1.
Handwritten internal operator architecture notes page 2.

work_packages.name: Aureon Evidence OS for controlled AI and grant operations
work_packages.formal_summary: A local-first evidence automation and AI workflow assurance system for controlled operator actions, source-grounded records, review gates, reproducible decision logs, and funder-ready evidence packs.
grant_ready_capabilities: controlled browser and portal workflow support
action proposal, review and logging controls
provider response schema and payload governance
do_not_claim: Do not claim autonomous final legal submission without operator approval and portal evidence.
Do not claim 100 percent grant success.
Do not present internal notes as an external independent validation record.

work_packages.name: HNC coherence and validation framework for AI-driven discovery
work_packages.formal_summary: A hypothesis-led research and software framework for coherence scoring, signal-processing experiments, traceability, replication planning, and falsification-oriented validation.
do_not_claim: Do not imply peer-reviewed proof or independent validation unless separately evidenced.
Do not use metaphysical or certainty language in funder documents.

work_packages.name: AI-assisted compliance and SME evidence automation
work_packages.formal_summary: A back-office evidence workflow for accounting support, compliance preparation, audit trails, document assembly, and human-review boundaries around regulated decisions.
grant_ready_capabilities: human review barrier for regulated decisions
do_not_claim: Do not copy private finance files into funder attachments by default.
Do not claim replacement of regulated professional judgement.

work_packages.name: Engineering, hardware and quantum-adjacent evidence workflow
work_packages.formal_summary: Early-stage engineering and signal-processing research that can support feasibility studies, test planning, and reproducibility evidence for a qualified hardware or research lead.
do_not_claim: Do not claim an eligible quantum hardware lead, facility access, or hardware consortium from local research alone.
Do not enter hardware budgets without partner evidence.

work_packages.name: Deployment-trial evidence assurance for partner-led sectors
work_packages.formal_summary: An evidence-management role for partner-led trials in maritime, energy, agriculture, automotive or public-sector commercialisation routes where an eligible lead owns the site, asset, permissions, budget and delivery plan.
do_not_claim: Do not claim vessel, port, farm, manufacturing, public-sector research organisation or Swiss partner access unless documented.
Do not start final IFS submissions for partner-led routes from internal evidence alone.

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: Git push governance / controlled git commit
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: source-control governance with human review barrier
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for software quality, traceability and reproducibility controls.

operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: dual-entry evidence and action audit ledger
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for auditability, quality assurance and operator accountability.

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: Provider gateway circuit / provider response schema
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: external-provider integration gateway with schema validation
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for safe integration with third-party services and repeatable data capture.

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: Global write kill switch / workspace guard
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: write-safety control and workspace boundary guard
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for risk management and controlled automation.

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: Proposal preview and action proposal safe warning
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: operator preview, risk warning and approval workflow
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for human-in-the-loop governance and external action control.

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: Patch age, rollback, human patch barrier, controlled test
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: change-control workflow with rollback, review barrier and validation tests

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: MCP, async IO orchestration, feedback and auto recovery loop
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: model-context integration, asynchronous orchestration, feedback and recovery controls
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for technical architecture where source evidence exists.

operator_term_taxonomy.internal_term: Local agent loop / provider agent loop
operator_term_taxonomy.grant_ready_term: local and external-agent workflow loops with audit logging
operator_term_taxonomy.formal_use: Use for process automation architecture, not as a claim of unsupervised legal action.

controls: This artifact translates local research and internal operator notes into funder-safe metadata.
It is not itself an external attachment unless a later pack selects and sanitizes specific sections.
No portal field, submission, upload, partner invite, award term or email action was performed.
Private finance files, passwords, tax identifiers and bank data are excluded by default.
No staged IFS route is marked submit-ready from research metadata alone.
